SEARCH
Package de.uka.ilkd.key.settings

Class SettingsConverter

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.settings.SettingsConverter
public final class SettingsConverter extends Object
Utility class providing various methods to read properties.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static String
    decode(@NonNull String str)
    Decode the given String according to the following rules: The String has to start with PREFIX and end with POSTFIX, otherwise a RuntimeException is thrown. Each occurrence of a String in the 2nd column of ENCODINGS is replaced by the corresponding element in the first column of ENCODINGS. The Strings in ENCODINGS are replaced from back to front, i.e.

    decodeString(String str)
    Replace occurrences of Strings in ENCODINGS by their corresponding decoding String.

    Encode a String according to the following rules: The encoded String is prefixed with PREFIX and suffixed with POSTFIX.

  • Method Details

    • encodeString

      public static String encodeString(String str)
      Replace occurrences of Strings in ENCODINGS by their corresponding encoding String.
      Parameters:
      str - the String to be encoded
      Returns:
      the encoded String

    • decodeString

      public static String decodeString(String str)
      Replace occurrences of Strings in ENCODINGS by their corresponding decoding String.
      Parameters:
      str - the String to be decoded
      Returns:
      the decoded String

    • decode

      public static String decode(@NonNull String str)
      Decode the given String according to the following rules:
      • The String has to start with PREFIX and end with POSTFIX, otherwise a RuntimeException is thrown.
      • Each occurrence of a String in the 2nd column of ENCODINGS is replaced by the corresponding element in the first column of ENCODINGS.
      • The Strings in ENCODINGS are replaced from back to front, i.e. first each occurrence of "#comma" is replaced by ",", then each "#backslash" by "\\\\" and so on until finally each "#hash" is replaced by "#".
      Parameters:
      str - the String to decode
      Returns:
      the decoded version of str

    • encode

      public static String encode(@NonNull String str)
      Encode a String according to the following rules:
      • The encoded String is prefixed with PREFIX and suffixed with POSTFIX.
      • Each occurrence of a String in the 1st column of ENCODINGS is replaced by the corresponding element in the first column of ENCODINGS.
      • The Strings in ENCODINGS are replaced from front to back, i.e. first each occurrence of "#" is replaced by "#hash", then each "\n" is replaced by "#newline" and so on until finally each "," is replaced by "#comma".
      Parameters:
      str - the String to encode
      Returns:
      the encoded version of str

    • read

      public static String read(Properties props, String key, String defaultVal)
      Read a String property and replace ENCODINGSs by the value they encode (see decode(String).
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to be read
      key - the properties object's key to be read
      defaultVal - the default value to return if the key is not found
      Returns:
      the encoded value of the given key if found, otherwise the given defaultVal

    • unsupportedPropertiesKeys

      public static Collection<String> unsupportedPropertiesKeys(Properties props, String... supportedKeys)
      Checks whether the given properties contain a key whose String representation is not contained in the given set of supported keys. The returned list is empty iff all the properties object's keys have String representations in supportedKeys.
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object whose keys are to check
      supportedKeys - the allowed String representations of keys in the given props
      Returns:
      the list of String representations of unsupported keys

    • unsupportedPropertiesKeysInSubSections

      public static Collection<String> unsupportedPropertiesKeysInSubSections(Configuration config, String[] supportedKeys)

    • readRawString

      public static String readRawString(Properties props, String key, String defaultValue)
      Read a raw String property without paying attention to ENCODINGSs. The read value will thus be returned without any changes.
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to be read
      key - the properties object's key to be read
      defaultValue - the default value to return if the key is not found
      Returns:
      the read String value

    • readRawStringList

      public static String[] readRawStringList(Properties props, String key, String split, String[] defaultValue)
      Read a list of raw Strings (see readRawString(Properties, String, String)).
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to be read
      key - the properties object's key to be read
      split - the String used for splitting the list values
      defaultValue - the default value to return if the key is not found
      Returns:
      the given key's value split into a list at occurrences of split

    • readFile

      public static String readFile(Properties props, String key, String defaultVal, ClassLoader loader)
      Read a file path from a file path in the properties object. If the key is not found, the default value will be returned. Otherwise, the value is interpreted as a file path and the corresponding file is searched using the given class loader. If the file is found, it's content is returned as a String. If something goes wrong (e.g. file is not found, file cannot be read), the default value is returned.
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to read
      key - the key whose value is to be read as an int
      defaultVal - the default value to read if the key isn't found or the corresponding file cannot be found/read
      loader - the ClassLoader to use for searching the file
      Returns:
      the read file content

    • read

      public static int read(Properties props, String key, int defaultVal)
      Read an integer value. If the key's value cannot be parsed into int, the default value will be returned.
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to read
      key - the key whose value is to be read as an int
      defaultVal - the default value to read if the key isn't found or cannot be parsed into int
      Returns:
      the read int value

    • read

      public static long read(Properties props, String key, long defaultVal)
      Read a long value. If the key's value cannot be parsed into long, the default value will be returned.
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to read
      key - the key whose value is to be read as a long
      defaultVal - the default value to read if the key isn't found or cannot be parsed into long
      Returns:
      the read long value

    • read

      public static boolean read(Properties props, String key, boolean defaultVal)
      Read a boolean. If the value is "false", return false. If the value is "true", return true. Otherwise, return the default value. Note that this is case-sensitive (e.g. "FALSE" and "True" lead to the default value being returned).
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to read
      key - the key whose value is to be read as a boolean. The value has to be one of "true", "false" (case-sensitive).
      defaultVal - the default value to read if the key is not found or doesn't equal "true" or "false"
      Returns:
      true iff the read value is "true", false iff the read value is "false", defaultVal otherwise

    • read

      public static String[] read(Properties props, String key, String[] defaultVal)
      Read a String list. The elements are assumed to be separated by LIST_SEPARATOR. The read Strings are encoded using decode(String).
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to read
      key - the key whose value is to be read as a String list
      defaultVal - the default list to read if the key is not found
      Returns:
      the read String list with separately encoded elements

    • store

      public static void store(Properties props, String key, String[] values)
      Store a String list. The list elements are separated using LIST_SEPARATOR.
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to write
      key - the key whose value is to be stored
      values - the array to store as a list

    • store

      public static void store(Properties props, String key, String value)
      Store a String value after decoding it with encode(String).
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to write
      key - the key whose value is to be stored
      value - the String value to store for key

    • store

      public static void store(Properties props, String key, boolean value)
      Store a boolean value.
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to write
      key - the key whose value is to be stored
      value - the boolean value to store for key

    • store

      public static void store(Properties props, String key, long value)
      Store a long value.
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to write
      key - the key whose value is to be stored
      value - the long value to store for key

    • read

      public static <T extends Enum<?>> T read(Properties props, String key, T defaultValue, T[] values)
      Read an enum constant property by its ordinal.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the enum which the read constant belongs to
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to read
      key - the key whose value is the enum constant's ordinal in enum T
      defaultValue - the default enum constant of T to return
      values - the enum values of T from which to choose the value to read
      Returns:
      the value of the read enum constant

    • store

      public static <T extends Enum<?>> void store(Properties props, String key, T value)
      Store an enum constant via its ordinal.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the enum which the stored constant belongs to
      Parameters:
      props - the properties object to write
      key - the key whose value is the enum constant's ordinal
      value - the enum constant of enum T which is to store
